opportunities.title: Lead intake and quote conversion
opportunities.customer_problem: Potential response-time, quote follow-up, and win/loss visibility gaps.
public_basis: APS public website advertises multiple inspection services and web/contact inquiry paths.
Building inspection services usually depend on fast trust-building and quote clarity.
opportunities.value_hypothesis: May improve responsiveness and quote follow-up once inquiry and quote data is approved.
opportunities.risk_if_wrong: If lead volume is low or already well-managed, this should be deferred.
opportunities.customer_confirmation_question: Should we model the inquiry-to-quote path first?

opportunities.title: Report production and QA/evidence library
opportunities.customer_problem: Report drafting, evidence consistency, and repeated defect language may be high-friction.
public_basis: APS value depends on inspection expertise, defect reporting, and client-understandable evidence.
The provided intake highlights past reports and photo libraries as high-value sources.
opportunities.value_hypothesis: May improve report consistency and reduce repeat drafting effort after source classification.
opportunities.risk_if_wrong: This touches sensitive property reports and photos, so privacy gates must be strongest here.
opportunities.customer_confirmation_question: Are reports and defect photos the highest-leverage knowledge source to process first?

opportunities.title: Booking and site-access preparation
opportunities.customer_problem: Access coordination, reminders, and missing pre-inspection information may create avoidable admin load.
public_basis: Inspection work is appointment and property-access dependent.
The building inspection lifecycle includes booking, site access, and preparation as required steps.
opportunities.value_hypothesis: May reduce scheduling friction and prevent access-related delays.
opportunities.risk_if_wrong: If bookings are already handled by a strong job system, Xlooop should integrate rather than replace it.
opportunities.customer_confirmation_question: Do booking and site-access issues create repeated admin friction today?

opportunities.title: Client questions and rectification follow-up
opportunities.customer_problem: Repeated client explanations, defect clarification, and builder/developer follow-up may be hard to track.
public_basis: Building inspection reports often generate follow-up questions and rectification conversations.
The provided intake names builder/developer rectification cycles and recurring client questions as discoverable from email.
opportunities.value_hypothesis: May turn repeated client support into reusable, evidence-linked guidance.
opportunities.risk_if_wrong: Legal or technical advice boundaries must be explicit before any agent assists.
opportunities.customer_confirmation_question: Should Xlooop help turn repeated client questions into guided follow-up and reusable answers?

opportunities.title: Payments, profitability, and cash conversion
opportunities.customer_problem: Job profitability, invoice timing, and payment delays may be invisible without accounting/payment data.
public_basis: Inspection services are job-based and have service-specific revenue and cost patterns.
The provided intake identifies Xero and Stripe as sources for revenue, profitability, AR ageing, and payments.
opportunities.value_hypothesis: May identify cash-conversion and service-margin patterns after accounting access is approved.
opportunities.risk_if_wrong: Financial access is sensitive and should be requested only after operational value is clear.
opportunities.customer_confirmation_question: Is payment and profitability visibility a priority after operational workflow mapping?

Do not launch GitHub remote before repo ownership and export/transfer policy are accepted.
Do not show fake APS operations activity.
Do not treat APS onboarding as Xlooop commercial traction before claim review.
